*Section One: Core Logic*
The C++ application generates a fractal-like grid where each cell holds a computed value based on Fibonacci and prime sequences. This approach demands efficient memory management and recursive algorithms to handle large datasets without overflow.

*Section Two: Pattern Rendering*
Visual output is crafted using dynamic string formatting. The program interleaves numeric data with geometric symbols, creating a dense tapestry of information that shifts based on user-defined parameters. No simple loops suffice here; we utilize lambda functions and iterators.

*Section Three: Complexity Analysis*
Performance metrics show that the algorithm operates within O(n log n) time complexity. This efficiency is achieved through advanced data structures like balanced trees and hash maps, ensuring the pattern remains stable even as input scales significantly.
